---
title: "testing_drift_detection"
output: html_document
---


```{r}
library(tidyverse)
library(arrow)
library(here)
library(patchwork)
library(ross.wq.tools)
```

# Read in verified data from 2025

```{r}
files <- list.files(path = here("data/raw/sensor/manual_data_verification/2025_cycle/post_verification"), pattern = "Turbidity|FDOM", full.names = T)

data <- map(files, ~read_parquet(.x)) %>%
  bind_rows()

#load_field visits
field_notes <- grab_mWater_sensor_notes(mWater_api_data = load_mWater())

```

## Current Drift Function
Copied from `ross.wq.tools`

```{r}
# Progressive drift helper
  progressive_drift <- function(x) {
    #Don't calculate if there are less than 4 points or less than 90% of data is present
    if(length(x) < 10) return(0)
    if (sum(!is.na(x)) < ceiling(0.9 * length(x))) return(0)
    #Compute linear model & extract slope
    model <- lm(x ~ seq_along(x), na.action = na.omit)
    slope <- coef(model)[2]

    if (param != "FDOM Fluorescence" && slope <= 0) return(0)
    if (param == "FDOM Fluorescence" && slope >  0) return(0)
    #extract r^2
    summary(model)$r.squared
  }
#updated to return slope too
progressive_drift <- function(x, param_name) {
  # 1. Don't calculate if there are less than 10 points or less than 90% data present
  if(length(x) < 10 || sum(!is.na(x)) < ceiling(0.9 * length(x))) {
    return(list(slope = 0, r2 = 0))
  }
  
  # 2. Compute linear model
  model <- lm(x ~ seq_along(x), na.action = na.omit)
  s <- as.numeric(coef(model)[2])
  r <- summary(model)$r.squared
  
  # 3. Apply your FDOM directional logic
  is_fdom <- (param_name == "FDOM Fluorescence")
  
  # Logic: If it's NOT FDOM and slope is negative/zero, OR if it IS FDOM and slope is positive...
  if ((!is_fdom && s <= 0) || (is_fdom && s > 0)) {
    return(list(slope = 0, r2 = 0))
  }
  
  return(list(slope = s, r2 = r))
}


exponential_drift <- function(x) {
  # no expontential drift for FDOM as it typically decreases with biofouling, so return 0 for all FDOM windows
  if (param == "FDOM Fluorescence") return(0)
  if(sum(!is.na(x)) < 10) return(0)
  # Avoid log(0) issues by shifting data slightly
  x_shifted <- x - min(x, na.rm = TRUE) + 0.1
  # Log-transform the turbidity to linearize the exponential
  log_y <- log(x_shifted)
  model <- lm(log_y ~ seq_along(x), na.action = na.omit)
  
  # Only return if the slope is positive (increasing fouling)
  if(coef(model)[2] <= 0) return(0)
  
  summary(model)$r.squared
}


  too_steady <- function(x) mean(x, na.rm = TRUE) >= 0.5

  # Regex to identify site visit flags
  visit_regex <- "(site\\s*visit|\\bsv\\b)"


  add_drift_flag <- function(df) {
    
    if (!unique(df$parameter) %in% c("FDOM Fluorescence", "Turbidity", "Chl-a Fluorescence")) {
      return(df)
    }
    
    param <- unique(df$parameter)[1]
    site_oi <- unique(df$site)[1]
    
    
    # Rolling window sizes
    win_short <- 96L   # ~1 day
    win_long  <- 288L  # ~3 days
    
    # Implementation
    new <- df %>%
      generate_summary_statistics(.)%>%
      arrange(DT_round)%>%
      #Split data by site visits to avoid crossing cleaning events
      mutate(visit_group = cumsum(str_detect(replace_na(flag, ""), regex(visit_regex, ignore_case = TRUE)))) %>%
      group_by(visit_group)%>%
      # Calculate R-squared values for different window sizes and alignments
      # 1 day = 1440 minutes
      # 3 days = 4320 minutes
      dplyr::mutate(
        # 1-day windows with different alignments
        res_s_center = slider::slide_index(mean, DT_round, .f = ~progressive_drift(.x, param), 
                                           .before = minutes(720), .after = minutes(720)),
        res_s_right  = slider::slide_index(mean, DT_round, .f = ~progressive_drift(.x, param), 
                                           .before = minutes(1440)),
        res_l_center = slider::slide_index(mean, DT_round, .f = ~progressive_drift(.x, param), 
                                           .before = minutes(2160), .after = minutes(2160)),
        res_l_right  = slider::slide_index(mean, DT_round, .f = ~progressive_drift(.x, param), 
                                           .before = minutes(2160)))%>%
      unnest_wider(res_s_center, names_sep = "_") %>%
      unnest_wider(res_s_right,  names_sep = "_") %>%
      unnest_wider(res_l_center, names_sep = "_") %>%
      unnest_wider(res_l_right,  names_sep = "_")%>%
      mutate(
        #Exponential
        r2_l_right_exp = slider::slide_index_dbl(.x = mean, .i = DT_round, .f = exponential_drift,
                                                 .before = minutes(4320)),
        r2_l_center_exp = slider::slide_index_dbl(.x = mean, .i = DT_round, .f = exponential_drift,
                                                  .before = minutes(2160), .after = minutes(2160)),
        #  # Take the maximum R-squared from any window configuration
        tightest_r = pmax(!!!pick(contains("r2")), na.rm = TRUE),      # Check if any 1-day period has consistently high R-squared values
        failed = slider::slide_index_dbl(.x = tightest_r, .i = DT_round, .before = minutes(1440), .f = too_steady)
      )%>%
      ungroup()%>%
      # Unnest the results into separate columns
      
      add_flag(failed == 1, "drift")
    
    cat(paste0("Completed drift calculations for ", site_oi, " - ", param, "\n"))
    
    return(new)
    
  }
```

# Run on data

```{r}

final <- data%>%
  #only keep turbidity
  filter(parameter == "Turbidity")%>%
  mutate(drift_flagged = if_else(correction_type %in% c("exponential", "linear"), "1", "0"))%>%
  split(f = list(.$site), sep = "-") %>%
  map(., ~add_drift_flag(.))%>%
  bind_rows()

```



```{r}

pred_df <- final%>%
   mutate(drift_perf = case_when(
    drift_flagged == "1" & flag == "drift" ~ "True Positive",
    drift_flagged == "0" & flag == "drift" ~ "False Positive",
    drift_flagged == "1" & (is.na(flag) | flag != "drift") ~ "False Negative",
    drift_flagged == "0" & (is.na(flag) | flag != "drift") ~ "True Negative"
  ))%>%
  mutate(target_cat = factor(drift_flagged, levels = c("1", "0"), labels = c("Drift", "No Drift")),
         pred_cat = factor(if_else(flag == "drift", "1", "0", missing = "0"), levels = c("1", "0"), labels = c("Drift", "No Drift")))
  

  conf_plot <- function(pred_df, plot_title,units) {
    if (is.null(pred_df)) return(NULL)

    df <- pred_df %>%
      mutate(pred_cat = factor(pred_cat, levels = levels(target_cat)))
    #compute performance metrics
    conf_table <- conf_mat(df, truth = target_cat, estimate = pred_cat, dnn = c("predicted", "truth"))
    performance <- summary(conf_table)

    acc  <- performance %>% filter(.metric == "accuracy") %>% pull(.estimate)
    kap  <- performance %>% filter(.metric == "kap") %>% pull(.estimate)
    f1   <- performance %>% filter(.metric == "f_meas") %>% pull(.estimate)

    conf_df <- conf_table$table %>%
      as_tibble() %>%
      mutate(
        value_perc = replace_na(n / sum(n), 0),
        True_lab = factor(truth, levels = levels(df$target_cat)),
        Pred_lab = factor(predicted, levels = levels(df$pred_cat))
      )

    perc_corr <- conf_df %>%
      group_by(True_lab) %>%
      summarize(percent_corr = round(sum(n[True_lab == Pred_lab]) /
                                       sum(n) * 100, 1)) %>%
      ungroup()

    ggplot(conf_df, aes(x = True_lab, y = Pred_lab, fill = value_perc)) +
      geom_tile(color = grey(0.6), size = 0.2) +
      geom_tile(data = filter(conf_df, True_lab == Pred_lab),
                color = "black", fill = "transparent", size = 1) +
      # scale_fill_gradient(low = "white", high = "blue",
      #                     labels = value_perc, limits = c(0, 1)) +
      geom_text(aes(label = n), size = 4) +
      geom_text(
        data = perc_corr,
        aes(x = True_lab, y = length(unique(conf_df$Pred_lab)) + 0.25,
            label = paste0("Acc: ", percent_corr, "%")),
        inherit.aes = FALSE,
        color = "red", size = 4, fontface = "bold"
      ) +
      labs(
        x = paste0("True Class (", units, ")"),
        y = paste0("Predicted Class (", units, ")"),
        title = plot_title,
        subtitle = paste0("Accuracy ", round(100 * acc, 1),
                          "%  F1: ", round(f1, 2),
                          "  Kappa: ", round(kap, 2)),
        fill = "Percent Correct"
      ) +
      theme_bw() +
      theme(
        legend.position = "bottom",
        panel.grid = element_blank(),
        axis.text = element_text(size = 11, color = "black"),
        axis.title = element_text(size = 12),
        axis.ticks = element_blank()
      )
  }


org_plot <- conf_plot(pred_df, plot_title = paste0("Overall Confusion Matrix, R2 Threshold = 0.5"), units = "Drift Label")

```

## Basic ML

```{r}
apply_training_scale <- function( new_data, full_dataset = NULL, scaling_params = NULL, features, scaling_param_out_path = NULL){
  `%nin%` <- Negate(`%in%`)
  #Check that new data  have all the features
  missing_in_new_data <- features[features %nin% colnames(new_data)]
  if(length(missing_in_new_data) > 0){
    stop(paste("The following features are missing in new_data:", paste(missing_in_new_data, collapse = ", ")))
  }
  #Only one of full_dataset or scaling_params should be provided and they both can't be NULL
  if(is.null(full_dataset) & is.null(scaling_params)){
    stop("Either full_dataset or scaling_params must be provided.")
  }

  #if no scaling params provided, compute from full dataset
  if(is_null(scaling_params)){
    #Check that full dataset has all the features
    missing_in_complete_data <- features[features %nin% colnames(full_dataset)]
    if(length(missing_in_complete_data) > 0){
      stop(paste("The following features are missing in full_dataset:", paste(missing_in_complete_data, collapse = ", ")))
    }

    scaling_params <- full_dataset %>%
      select(all_of(features)) %>% #only scale features
      #make sure all features are numeric
      mutate(across(everything(), ~ as.numeric(.x))) %>%
      #compute min and max for each feature
      summarise(across(
        everything(),
        list(min = ~min(.x, na.rm = TRUE), max = ~max(.x, na.rm = TRUE)), #get min and max
        .names = "{.col}_{.fn}"
      ))
  }else{
    #check that scaling parameters has all required columns
    required_cols <- unlist(lapply(features, function(x) c(paste0(x, "_min"), paste0(x, "_max"))))
    missing_in_scaling_params <- required_cols[required_cols %nin% colnames(scaling_params)]
    if(length(missing_in_scaling_params) > 0){
      stop(paste("The following scaling parameter columns are missing in scaling_params:", paste(missing_in_scaling_params, collapse = ", ")))
    }
  }

  #if scaling params output dir provided, save scaling params as parquet file
  if(!is.null(scaling_param_out_path)){
    # Get the directory
    scaling_param_out_dir <- dirname(scaling_param_out_path)
    #check to see if dir exists & create if not
    if(!dir.exists(scaling_param_out_dir)){
      dir.create(scaling_param_out_dir, recursive = TRUE)
    }

    scaling_params %>%
      write_parquet(here(paste0(scaling_param_out_path, ".parquet")))
  }

  #apply new scaling to new data
  scaled_data <- new_data %>%
    #make sure all features are numeric
    mutate(across(all_of(features), ~ as.numeric(.x))) %>%
    #apply scaling
    mutate(across(
      all_of(features),
      ~ {
        col <- cur_column()
        #get scaling from complete sensor data
        min_val <- scaling_params[[paste0(col, "_min")]]
        max_val <- scaling_params[[paste0(col, "_max")]]
        #rescale the original data to 0-1 based on training data min/max
        scales::rescale(.x, to = c(0, 1), from = c(min_val, max_val))
      },
      .names = "{.col}" # overwrite with scaled values (keep same names)
    ))

  return(scaled_data)

}

```
Split data by site for validation and test



```{r}
all_data <- final%>%
  mutate(hours_since_cleaning = difftime(DT_round, last_site_visit, units = "hours"))%>%
  select(c(DT_round, site,mean,auto_flag, contains("_r2"), contains("_slope"), rollsd, rollmed,rollavg,tightest_r ,hours_since_cleaning, drift_flagged))%>%
  #set drift flagged as factor for classification
  mutate(drift_cat = as.numeric(as.factor(drift_flagged))-1)

target_col = "drift_cat"
  features <- setdiff(names(all_data), c(target_col, "site", "drift_flagged", "mean", "DT_round","auto_flag"))


all_data_norm <- all_data%>%
  apply_training_scale(
    new_data = .,
    full_dataset = .,
    features = features
  )%>%
  select( -drift_flagged)
  
train_data <- all_data_norm %>%
  filter(site %in% c( "archery" ,"bellvue","cbri","chd","cottonwood", "joei","pbd","pbr","pfal","pman","riverbluffs"))

val_data <- all_data_norm %>%
  filter(site %in% c("riverbend", "elc","salyer"))

test <- all_data_norm %>%
  filter(site %in% c("sfm","udall"))


nrow(train_data)/nrow(all_data)
nrow(val_data)/nrow(all_data)
nrow(test)/nrow(all_data)
```


```{r}
#train xgboost model on train data and validate on validation data, using the R2 values and hours since cleaning as predictors for drift_flagged
library(xgboost)

    booster_params <- list(
      objective        = "multi:softprob",
      eval_metric      = "mlogloss",
      eta              = 0.01,
      gamma            = 0.8,
      alpha            = 1,
      lambda           = 1,
      max_depth        = 5,
      subsample        = 0.8,
      colsample_bytree = 0.5,
      min_child_weight  = 6,
      num_class        = 2
    )

    weights = function(df) {
      ifelse(df$drift_cat == 1, 3,1) 
    }
    
    w_train <- weights(train_data)
    w_val   <- weights(val_data)
    
    
    
    # ---- Set up data matrix for xgb ----
    dtrain <- xgb.DMatrix(
      data = as.matrix(train_data[, features]),
      label = train_data[[target_col]],
      weight = w_train # add weights
    )
    
    dval <- xgb.DMatrix(
      data = as.matrix(val_data[, features]),
      label = val_data[[target_col]],
      weight = w_val # add weights
    )

    # Set train vs val for early stopping
    watchlist <- list(train = dtrain, eval = dval)
    set.seed(123)
    
    #train model with hyper parameters
    model <- xgb.train(
      params = booster_params,
      data = dtrain,
      nrounds = 2000,
      watchlist = watchlist,
      #change early stopping rounds based on eta (smaller eta, larger rounds)
      early_stopping_rounds = 100,
      print_every_n = 100,
      verbose = 1,
      nthread = 1
    )

```

## Check out performance in TV space

```{r}

org_plot + plot_tvt_fold_acc(model,train_df = train_data, fold_num = 1, val_df = val_data, test_df = NULL,
                              target_col = "drift_cat", units = "", title = "",
                              class_levels = c("No Drift", "Drift"))%>%
  wrap_plots(guides = "collect")

```


```{r}
xgb.importance(model = model)
```

# Apply preds in TS space

```{r}
predict_classes <- function(df, pred_col, target_col) {
    if(is.null(df)){
      return(NULL)
    }

    preds <- predict(model, as.matrix(df[, model$feature_names]))

    if(model$params$objective == "multi:softprob"){
      preds_vector <- max.col(matrix(preds, ncol = length(class_levels), byrow = TRUE)) - 1
    }else{
      preds_vector <- preds
    }
    df <- df %>%
      #if softprob, convert probs to class labels (max prob)
      mutate(!!pred_col := preds_vector)
    #convert to factors with levels
    df %>%
      mutate(
        target_cat = factor(.data[[target_col]],
                            levels = seq_along(class_levels) - 1,
                            labels = class_levels),
        pred_cat = factor(.data[[pred_col]],
                          levels = seq_along(class_levels) - 1,
                          labels = class_levels)
      )
  }

train_data_with_preds <- predict_classes(train_data, pred_col = "drift_pred", target_col = target_col)
val_data_with_preds <- predict_classes(val_data, pred_col = "drift_pred", target_col = target_col)
```

```{r}

  visit_regex <- "(site\\s*visit|\\bsv\\b)"

plot_data <- bind_rows(train_data_with_preds, val_data_with_preds) %>%
  group_by(site) %>%
  mutate(
    # Create groups based on site visits
    visit_group = cumsum(str_detect(replace_na(auto_flag, ""), regex(visit_regex, ignore_case = TRUE))),
    dataset = if_else(site %in% train_data$site, "Train", "Validation"),
    network = if_else(site %in% c("chd", "pbr", "pfal","pman", "pbd", "sfm", "cbri", "joei"), "Upper", "Lower")
  ) %>%
  group_by(site, visit_group) %>%
  arrange(DT_round) %>% # Ensure chronological order for rolling windows
  
  # 2. PASS 1: Remove isolated drift predictions (blips)
  # If a 2-hour window has < 25% drift hits, treat isolated 1s as noise (0)
  mutate(
    drift_density = zoo::rollapply(drift_pred, width = 8, FUN = function(x) sum(x, na.rm = TRUE) / length(x), 
                                   fill = NA, align = "center"),
    # Update drift_pred: If it was a 1 but density is very low, it's likely an isolated false hit
    drift_pred_clean = if_else(drift_pred == 1 & drift_density < 0.25, 0, drift_pred)
  ) %>%

  # 3. PASS 2: Flag isolated data points within mostly missing/flagged regions
  mutate(
    # Binary for missing or already suspect data
    auto_flag_binary = if_else(is.na(mean) | is.na(drift_pred_clean), 1, 0),
    
    # Check for 2-hour windows with >= 90% missing data
    missing_density_center = zoo::rollapply(auto_flag_binary, width = 8, 
                                            FUN = function(x) sum(x) / length(x), 
                                            fill = NA, align = "center"),
    
    # If a point is valid but surrounded by missing data, mark it suspect
    is_isolated = if_else(!is.na(mean) & missing_density_center >= (8/9), TRUE, FALSE)
  ) %>%
  
  # 4. Final Classification Logic
  mutate(
    # Nullify predictions for isolated points
    drift_pred_final = if_else(is_isolated, NA_real_, drift_pred_clean),
    
    # Recalculate performance metrics based on cleaned predictions
    drift_perf = case_when(
      drift_cat == 1 & drift_pred_final == 1 ~ "True Positive",
      drift_cat == 0 & drift_pred_final == 1 ~ "False Positive",
      drift_cat == 1 & (is.na(drift_pred_final) | drift_pred_final != 1) ~ "False Negative",
      drift_cat == 0 & (is.na(drift_pred_final) | drift_pred_final != 1) ~ "True Negative"
    )
  ) %>%
  ungroup()
```


```{r}
plot_data <- bind_rows(train_data_with_preds, val_data_with_preds) %>%
  mutate(dataset = if_else(site %in% train_data$site, "Train", "Validation"), 
         drift_perf = case_when(
           drift_cat == 1 & drift_pred == 1 ~ "True Positive",
           drift_cat == 0 & drift_pred == 1 ~ "False Positive",
           drift_cat == 1 & (is.na(drift_pred) | drift_pred != 1) ~ "False Negative",
           drift_cat == 0 & (is.na(drift_pred) | drift_pred != 1) ~ "True Negative"
         ), 
         network = if_else(site %in% c("chd", "pbr", "pfal","pman", "pbd", "sfm", "cbri", "joei"), "Upper", "Lower"))

ggplot(plot_data%>%filter(network == "Lower"), aes(x = DT_round, y = mean, color = drift_perf)) +
  geom_point(size = 2) +
  facet_wrap(~site, ncol = 1, scale = "free_y") +
  ylim(0, 300)+
  scale_color_manual(values = c("True Positive" = "green4", "False Positive" = "red3",
                                "False Negative" = "orange2", "True Negative" = "skyblue"), name = "Drift Detection Performance")
```


## Network Check

Historically, drift was not removed from network check but let's see how we do if it is included

```{r}
threshold_value <- 0.5

intrasensor_flags_list <- final%>%
  #remove previous flags
  mutate(  network = if_else(site %in% c("chd", "pbr", "pfal","pman", "pbd", "sfm", "cbri", "joei"), "Upper", "Lower"), 
           old_flag = flag,
          flag = NA)%>%
  add_flag(mean_steady_r >= threshold_value, "drift_detect")%>%
  mutate(drift_perf = case_when(
    drift_flagged == "1" & flag == "drift_detect" ~ "True Positive",
    drift_flagged == "0" & flag == "drift_detect" ~ "False Positive",
    drift_flagged == "1" & (is.na(flag) | flag != "drift_detect") ~ "False Negative",
    drift_flagged == "0" & (is.na(flag) | flag != "drift_detect") ~ "True Negative"
  ))%>%
  select(site, parameter,network, DT_round,  mean, mean_analysis,old_flag, user_flag, flag, drift_flagged,drift_perf)%>%
  split(f = list(.$site, .$parameter), sep = "-")


#make a confusion matrix for each site of how the drift detection performed before network check

conf_matrix_data <- bind_rows(intrasensor_flags_list) %>%
  mutate(
    # Clean up labels for the legend
    Observed = factor(drift_flagged, levels = c("1", "0"), labels = c("Drift", "No Drift")),
    Predicted = factor(if_else(flag == "drift_detect", "1", "0", missing = "0"), 
                       levels = c("1", "0"), labels = c("Drift", "No Drift"))
  )%>%
  group_by(site, Observed, Predicted) %>%
  tally() %>%
  # Group by site and Observed so the denominator is the total of each manual label
  group_by(site, Observed) %>% 
  mutate(percentage = n / sum(n) * 100)

# Create the Heatmap
ggplot(conf_matrix_data, aes(x = Observed , y = Predicted, fill = percentage)) +
  geom_tile() +
  geom_text(aes(label = paste0(n, "\n(", round(percentage, 1), "%)")), color = "white") +
  facet_wrap(~site) +
  scale_fill_viridis_c(option = "mako", direction = -1) +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom") +
  labs(
    title = paste0("Confusion Matrices: Observed vs. Predicted, R2 Threshold = ", threshold_value),
    subtitle = "Empirical check of Type 1 (FP) and Type 2 (FN) errors per site",
    x = "Observed (Manual)", 
    y = "Predicted ", 
    fill = "Present Correct for Category"
  )


pre_network_check_plot <- intrasensor_flags_list %>%
  bind_rows() %>%
  filter(network == "Lower")%>%
  ggplot(., aes(x = DT_round, y = mean_analysis, color = drift_perf)) +
  geom_point(size = 2) +
  facet_wrap(~site, ncol = 1, scale = "free_y") +
  labs(x = "Date", y = "Mean Turbidity", title = "Evaluation of Drift Detection without Network Check",
       subtitle = "True Positives (red) are correctly identified drift events, while False Positives (orange) are incorrect drift flags. \nFalse Negatives (blue) are missed drift events, and True Negatives (green) are correctly identified non-drift periods.") +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("True Positive" = "tomato", "False Positive" = "orange", "False Negative" = "steelblue", "True Negative" = "skyblue"), name = "Drift Detection Performance")

```




```{r}
site_order_list <- load_site_order(file_path = "data/derived/auto_qaqc_files/site_order/site_order_2025.yml")

network_flags <- intrasensor_flags_list %>%
  # network check compares patterns across sites
  purrr::map(~network_check(df = ., intrasensor_flags_arg = intrasensor_flags_list,
                            site_order_arg = site_order_list))%>%
  bind_rows()

post_network_check_plot <- network_flags%>%
  mutate(drift_perf = case_when(
    drift_flagged == "1" & auto_flag == "drift_detect" ~ "True Positive",
    drift_flagged == "0" & auto_flag == "drift_detect" ~ "False Positive",
    drift_flagged == "1" & (is.na(auto_flag) | auto_flag != "drift_detect") ~ "False Negative",
    drift_flagged == "0" & (is.na(auto_flag) | auto_flag != "drift_detect") ~ "True Negative"
  ))%>%
  filter(network == "Lower")%>%
  ggplot(., aes(x = DT_round, y = mean_analysis, color = drift_perf)) +
  geom_point(size = 2) +
  facet_wrap(~site, ncol = 1, scale = "free_y") +
  labs(x = "Date", y = "Mean Turbidity", title = "Evaluation of Drift Detection with Network Check",
       subtitle = "True Positives (red) are correctly identified drift events, while False Positives (orange) are incorrect drift flags. \nFalse Negatives (blue) are missed drift events, and True Negatives (green) are correctly identified non-drift periods.") +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("True Positive" = "tomato", "False Positive" = "orange", "False Negative" = "steelblue", "True Negative" = "skyblue"), name = "Drift Detection Performance")

pre_network_check_plot + post_network_check_plot %>%
  wrap_plots(guides = "collect")


network_flags %>%
  mutate(
    Observed = factor(drift_flagged, levels = c("1", "0"), labels = c("Drift", "No Drift")),
    Predicted = factor(if_else(auto_flag == "drift_detect", "1", "0", missing = "0"), 
                       levels = c("1", "0"), labels = c("Drift", "No Drift"))
  )%>%
  group_by(site, Observed, Predicted) %>%
  tally() %>%
  group_by(site, Observed) %>% 
  mutate(percentage = n / sum(n) * 100)%>%
ggplot(., aes(x = Observed , y = Predicted, fill = percentage)) +
  geom_tile() +
  geom_text(aes(label = paste0(n, "\n(", round(percentage, 1), "%)")), color = "white") +
  facet_wrap(~site) +
  scale_fill_viridis_c(option = "mako", direction = -1) +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom") +
  labs(
    title = paste0("Confusion Matrices: Observed vs. Predicted, R2 Threshold = ", threshold_value),
    subtitle = "Post Network Check",
    x = "Observed (Manual)", 
    y = "Predicted ", 
    fill = "Present of Column for Category"
  )


pre_network_check_plot <- intrasensor_flags_list %>%
  bind_rows() %>%
  filter(network == "Lower")%>%
  ggplot(., aes(x = DT_round, y = mean_analysis, color = drift_perf)) +
  geom_point(size = 2) +
  facet_wrap(~site, ncol = 1, scale = "free_y") +
  labs(x = "Date", y = "Mean Turbidity", title = "Evaluation of Drift Detection without Network Check",
       subtitle = "True Positives (red) are correctly identified drift events, while False Positives (orange) are incorrect drift flags. \nFalse Negatives (blue) are missed drift events, and True Negatives (green) are correctly identified non-drift periods.") +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("True Positive" = "tomato", "False Positive" = "orange", "False Negative" = "steelblue", "True Negative" = "skyblue"), name = "Drift Detection Performance")
```
